title: afrocarpus falcatus subsp. gracilior
intro: Afrocarpus falcatus subsp. gracilior, also known as the Gracilior or East African Yellowwood, is a subspecies of tree that belongs to the family Podocarpaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its medicinal properties which include antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Afrocarpus falcatus subsp. gracilior include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and preventing infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant's bark and leaves can be prepared into teas, infusions, and extracts, which are then used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
